NES 2-bit subset: Classic constrained palette with inky black, deep navy blue, coral red, and warm cream for authentic retro authenticity.
Gameboy DMG green: Four iconic shades from dark forest green to pale mint, the nostalgic monochrome screen everyone remembers fondly.
Pico-8 warm classic: Charming 16-color fixed palette featuring peach, dusty red, indigo, teal, and yellow for cozy fantasy games.
CGA magenta-cyan: Stark 4-color IBM palette with pure black, electric cyan, hot magenta, and bright white for retro computing aesthetic.
Celeste mountain palette: Dreamy deep purple, soft peach, refreshing teal, and gentle cream capturing the mountain climbing adventure's emotional journey.
Hyper Light Drifter neon: Moody palette with rich magenta, electric teal, and vibrant orange creating atmospheric sci-fi post-apocalyptic worlds.
Dead Cells dungeon: Dark brooding purple, fiery orange, and off-white cream perfectly suited for roguelike metroidvania dungeon exploration.
Shovel Knight NES: Carefully curated NES-restricted palette using cobalt blue, gold yellow, forest green, and crimson for nostalgic platforming adventures.
Katana Zero cyberpunk: Electric neon palette featuring hot pink, cyan blue, deep purple, and stark white for stylish neo-noir action.
Undertale cozy limited: Warm minimal palette with cream, chocolate brown, cherry red, and golden yellow for heartfelt indie RPG storytelling.
SNES pastel dreamscape: Luminous bright cyan, playful magenta, sunny yellow, and pure white creating cheerful colorful 16-bit era visuals.
Stardew Valley farmstead: Earthy brown, sage green, dusty rose, and soft butter yellow evoking peaceful rural countryside pixel charm.
Atari 2600 constrained: Ultra-limited palette with burnt orange, tan beige, dark brown, and black for pioneering home console authenticity.
EGA 16-color IBM: Classic PC palette featuring royal blue, lime green, bright cyan, and vivid magenta for nostalgic DOS gaming.
Amiga Workbench 32: Rich palette with steel blue, soft gray, burnt orange, and white representing classic computing workstation aesthetic.
Commodore 64 limited: Iconic palette using violet purple, sky blue, lime green, and tan for beloved home computer pixel graphics.
PC-98 Japanese aesthetic: Gorgeous muted palette with dusty pink, slate blue, soft lavender, and cream for elegant visual novel art.
Chrono Trigger SNES: Vibrant time-travel palette featuring royal purple, emerald green, golden amber, and cherry red for epic RPG adventure.
Spelunky cave exploration: Earthy palette with deep brown, sandy tan, mossy green, and crimson red for procedural underground adventuring.
Hotline Miami psychedelic: Intense neon pink, electric purple, acid green, and stark black creating violent fever dream ultraviolence aesthetic.
Owlboy jewel-tone sky: Rich sapphire blue, emerald green, ruby red, and amber gold creating lush airborne cloud-world adventures.
Vampire Survivors gothic: Blood crimson, shadowy purple, pale moonlight white, and midnight black for endless supernatural monster-slaying hordes.
Fez geometric warm: Cozy burnt orange, sandy tan, sky blue, and cream white for perspective-shifting puzzle platformer exploration.
Enter the Gungeon bullet: Gunmetal gray, muzzle-flash orange, acid green, and ivory white for chaotic roguelike gunfight mayhem.
Game Boy Color pastel: Soft lavender, mint green, baby pink, and buttercream yellow for handheld gaming's colorful evolution.
Terraria adventure earth: Rich soil brown, stone gray, grass green, and azure blue for expansive 2D sandbox crafting worlds.
Mother 3 heartfelt: Warm brick red, sunshine yellow, sky blue, and gentle pink capturing quirky emotional Japanese RPG storytelling.
Sega Master System vibrant: Electric blue, hot magenta, lemon yellow, and forest green for bold 8-bit console gaming.
Atari Lynx handheld bright: Vivid orange, tropical teal, hot pink, and sunny yellow for portable gaming's most colorful screen.
VVVVVV minimal gravity: Stark cyan, crimson red, pure black, and bright white for challenging gravity-flipping precision platforming.
Octopath Traveler HD-2D: Rich modern palette with deep sapphire, burgundy wine, forest moss, and champagne gold for luxurious nostalgic RPGs.
NeoGeo Pocket candy: Vibrant pastel palette featuring bubblegum pink, mint aqua, lemon sorbet, and lavender for handheld sprite charm.
MSX 2 16-color retro: Classic Japanese computer palette with navy blue, tangerine orange, Kelly green, and rose pink for home programming.
Arcade cabinet primary: Bold vibrant palette using fire-engine red, electric yellow, cobalt blue, and pure white for coin-op excitement.
Stellaris sci-fi cool: Icy palette with steel blue, mint cyan, slate gray, and plasma violet for galactic strategy empire building.
Risk of Rain pixel: Stormy palette featuring thundercloud gray, lightning white, rain blue, and hazard orange for roguelike survival chaos.
Dark Souls dungeon gloom: Oppressive palette with charcoal black, ash gray, dim torch orange, and dried blood maroon for brutal exploration.
Thomas Was Alone minimal: Ultra-simple palette using slate gray, salmon pink, mint green, and cream for abstract geometric character storytelling.
CD-i pastel digital: Soft multimedia palette with periwinkle blue, coral peach, honeydew green, and vanilla cream for interactive video adventures.
Crossover modern expanded: Contemporary indie palette featuring plum purple, seafoam green, terracotta orange, and champagne for flexible pixel creation.
Sega Genesis blast processing: Bold electric blue, fire red, jungle green, and sunset gold for 16-bit speed and attitude.
ZX Spectrum rubber keys: Bright primary red, yellow, cyan, and magenta with stark black and white for British computing nostalgia.
Apple II hi-res: Vibrant violet, orange, lime green, and aqua blue creating the distinctive color fringing of early home computers.
Mega Man NES action: Cobalt blue, steel gray, energy beam cyan, and warning yellow for robotic platformer combat precision.
Super Metroid atmosphere: Alien purple, toxic green, molten orange, and cold steel blue for isolated planetary exploration dread.
Silent Hill fog: Oppressive rust brown, sickly yellow, blood crimson, and murky gray for psychological survival horror suffocation.
Kirby's Dream Land pink: Bubblegum pink, cotton candy white, cherry red, and sky blue for adorable puffball inhaling adventures.
Final Fantasy VI opera: Royal purple, golden yellow, rose red, and sage green for dramatic 16-bit RPG ensemble storytelling.
Castlevania gothic stone: Cathedral gray, candlelight amber, vampire crimson, and midnight navy for vampire-hunting platformer action.
Street Fighter II arcade: Championship gold, dragon red, hadouken blue, and tiger orange for competitive one-on-one fighting intensity.
